articles.headline: India Unveils National AI in Governance Strategy 2025: Focus on Ethical Deployment and Public Service Delivery
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the 'National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence in Governance (NSAIG) 2025', aiming to integrate AI across various government services.
• Focus areas include enhancing efficiency, transparency, and accessibility of public services, with a strong emphasis on ethical AI principles and data privacy.
• The strategy outlines a framework for responsible AI development, including guidelines for data acquisition, algorithm bias detection, and human oversight.
• Key sectors identified for initial implementation include healthcare, education, agriculture, and urban planning.
• A dedicated National AI Council, comprising experts from government, industry, and academia, will oversee the strategy's implementation and evolution.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Role of technology in governance
prelimsPoints: NSAIG 2025 launched for AI integration in government.
National AI Council established for oversight.
Initial focus sectors: healthcare, education, agriculture, urban planning.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of AI in transforming public service delivery in India, citing examples from NSAIG 2025.
Analyze the challenges of implementing AI in governance, particularly concerning ethical considerations, data privacy, and algorithmic bias.
Evaluate the role of a dedicated National AI Council in ensuring responsible AI development and deployment.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of AI algorithms connecting various government services.
articles.trivia: India is among the top 10 countries globally in terms of AI skill penetration.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Applications of AI in public administration

articles.headline: Gyalo Thondup, Elder Brother of 14th Dalai Lama, Passes Away at 97
articles.explainer:
• Gyalo Thondup, the elder brother of the 14th Dalai Lama, Tenzin Gyatso, passed away at the age of 97.
• He was a prominent figure in the Tibetan exile community and played a crucial role in early diplomatic efforts for Tibet after the Chinese invasion in 1950.
• Thondup served as the head of the Tibetan government-in-exile's foreign department for many years, engaging with international leaders and organizations.
• He was known for his pragmatic approach and his efforts to maintain communication channels with Beijing, advocating for a peaceful resolution to the Tibet issue.
• His extensive memoirs, 'The Noodle Maker of Kalimpong', offer unique insights into Tibetan history and the challenges faced by the exiled community.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues
India and its neighbourhood- relations
prelimsPoints: Gyalo Thondup was the elder brother of the 14th Dalai Lama.
Key figure in the Tibetan government-in-exile's foreign department.
Authored 'The Noodle Maker of Kalimpong'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical role of the Tibetan government-in-exile in preserving Tibetan culture and identity.
Analyze the complexities of India's policy towards Tibet and its implications for India-China relations.
Examine the 'Middle Way Approach' advocated by the Dalai Lama and its relevance in resolving the Tibet issue.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait of Gyalo Thondup in his later years.
articles.trivia: Kalimpong, mentioned in his memoir title, is a town in West Bengal, India, which became a significant hub for Tibetan refugees.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of GST on Online Gaming, Betting and Casinos
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India upheld the validity of the amendments to the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Act, 2017, imposing 28% GST on online gaming, betting, and casinos.
• The ruling clarified that the valuation for GST would be on the full face value of the bets placed, not on the net proceeds or platform fees.
• This decision brings clarity to the taxation regime for the burgeoning online gaming industry, which had challenged the retrospective application and valuation method.
• The court emphasized the legislative intent to treat these activities as 'actionable claims' for taxation purposes, distinct from skill-based games for revenue generation.
• The judgment is expected to significantly impact the revenue collection for the government and may lead to consolidation in the online gaming sector.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: SC upheld 28% GST on online gaming, betting, casinos.
GST valuation on full face value of bets.
Activities treated as 'actionable claims' for taxation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's decision on the Indian online gaming industry and its impact on revenue generation for the government.
Discuss the debate surrounding the distinction between 'games of skill' and 'games of chance' in the context of taxation and regulation in India.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities presented by the taxation of the digital economy, using the online gaming sector as an example.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating GST percentage on online gaming with court gavel.
articles.trivia: The global online gaming market is projected to reach over $300 billion by 2027.

articles.headline: India-Sri Lanka Joint Naval Exercise 'SLINEX 2025' Concludes, Enhancing Maritime Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The 13th edition of the bilateral maritime exercise 'SLINEX 2025' between India and Sri Lanka concluded in Visakhapatnam, focusing on interoperability and mutual understanding.
• The exercise included naval ships, maritime patrol aircraft, and special forces from both nations, conducting a range of drills from anti-piracy operations to HADR (Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief).
• SLINEX aims to strengthen the enduring friendship and enhance cooperation in maritime security in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• Discussions also focused on sharing best practices in maritime domain awareness (MDA) and addressing common challenges like illegal fishing and maritime terrorism.
• The exercise underscores India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and its commitment to Sagar (Security and Growth for All in the Region) doctrine.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: SLINEX is a bilateral maritime exercise between India and Sri Lanka.
13th edition concluded in Visakhapatnam.
Focus on HADR, anti-piracy, and MDA.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India-Sri Lanka defence cooperation for maritime security in the Indian Ocean Region.
Discuss how India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy is reflected in its defence engagements with littoral states.
Examine the common maritime security challenges faced by India and Sri Lanka and how joint exercises like SLINEX help address them.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and Sri Lankan naval ships sailing in formation during SLINEX.
articles.trivia: Sri Lanka is strategically located along major international shipping lanes in the Indian Ocean.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Cryogenic Engine for Future Heavy-Lift Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful hot test of its new high-thrust cryogenic engine, designed for future heavy-lift launch vehicles.
• This engine, designated 'CE-30', is a significant upgrade from the existing CE-20 used in GSLV Mk-III (LVM3), offering increased thrust and efficiency.
• The successful test marks a crucial milestone in India's ambition to enhance its capabilities for launching heavier communication satellites and deep-space missions.
• The CE-30 engine incorporates advanced materials and design methodologies, improving propellant efficiency and reducing overall launch costs.
• This development is vital for India's manned space mission, Gaganyaan, and for establishing a more robust presence in the commercial space launch market.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully hot-tested 'CE-30' cryogenic engine.
CE-30 is a next-gen engine for heavy-lift missions.
Significant for Gaganyaan and commercial space launches.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous cryogenic engine technology for India's space program and its strategic implications.
Analyze how advancements in launch vehicle technology, like the CE-30 engine, contribute to India's aspirations in deep-space exploration and manned missions.
Evaluate the potential impact of enhanced heavy-lift capabilities on India's position in the global commercial space market.
articles.imageDescription: Diagram of a cryogenic engine undergoing a hot test.
articles.trivia: Cryogenic engines use propellants (liquid oxygen and liquid hydrogen) stored at extremely low temperatures.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Reforms in Disaster Management Act, 2005
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report recommending significant amendments to the Disaster Management Act, 2005, to address emerging challenges.
• Key recommendations include integrating climate change adaptation and mitigation into disaster management planning and strengthening early warning systems.
• The panel called for greater emphasis on pre-disaster preparedness, risk reduction, and community-led initiatives, moving beyond a reactive relief-centric approach.
• It also suggested streamlining the National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) and State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) operations and improving inter-agency coordination.
• The report highlighted the need for a dedicated fund for disaster mitigation and capacity building at the local level, along with robust post-disaster recovery frameworks.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary panel recommended reforms for DMA 2005.
Focus on climate change, pre-disaster preparedness, community-led initiatives.
Suggested dedicated fund for mitigation and capacity building.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the effectiveness of the Disaster Management Act, 2005, in addressing contemporary disaster challenges in India.
Discuss the importance of shifting from a reactive to a proactive and holistic approach in disaster management, as recommended by the Parliamentary Panel.
Examine the role of community participation and local capacity building in enhancing disaster resilience in India.
articles.imageDescription: People evacuating during a flood, depicting disaster management scenario.
articles.trivia: India is highly vulnerable to various natural disasters, with over 58% of its landmass prone to earthquakes.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns and Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) maintained the benchmark repo rate at 6.5% for the seventh consecutive time.
• The decision was driven by persistent concerns over elevated retail inflation, particularly in food prices, and global economic uncertainties.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• The MPC's outlook highlighted risks from geopolitical tensions, volatility in crude oil prices, and potential impact on domestic economic activity.
• Analysts anticipate that the RBI will continue to adopt a cautious stance, closely monitoring inflation trajectory and global developments before considering any rate adjustments.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Monetary Policy
Inflation
prelimsPoints: RBI maintained repo rate at 6.5%.
Seventh consecutive time rate held steady.
Decision driven by inflation concerns and global uncertainties.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst current economic conditions, including inflation and global factors.
Discuss the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance of the RBI and its implications for economic growth and inflation control.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation management with the need to support economic recovery.
articles.imageDescription: RBI emblem over a graph showing stable repo rate.
articles.trivia: The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks in the event of any shortfall of funds.

articles.headline: India Pushes for Equitable Global Framework on Climate Finance at UN Climate Forum
articles.explainer:
• India advocated for a new, equitable global framework for climate finance at the ongoing UN Climate Forum, emphasizing the historical responsibility of developed nations.
• The Indian delegation highlighted the need for increased and predictable financial support for developing countries to achieve their climate mitigation and adaptation goals.
• India stressed that existing climate finance commitments, particularly the $100 billion per year pledge, have not been adequately met, hindering global climate action.
• The proposal included mechanisms for transparent tracking of climate finance flows and ensuring that funds are easily accessible and do not add to the debt burden of developing nations.
• This push aligns with India's long-standing position on 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in climate negotiations.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Environmental pollution and degradation
keyTerms: Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: India advocated for equitable global climate finance framework.
Emphasized historical responsibility of developed nations.
Highlighted failure to meet $100 billion climate finance pledge.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's stance on climate finance at international forums and the principles underlying its demands.
Analyze the challenges in mobilizing and disbursing adequate climate finance to developing countries and the implications for global climate action.
Evaluate the concept of 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in the context of global climate negotiations and its relevance today.
articles.imageDescription: Delegates discussing at a UN climate conference table.
articles.trivia: The $100 billion climate finance goal was first pledged by developed countries at COP15 in Copenhagen in 2009.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Semiconductor India 2.0' Policy to Boost Domestic Manufacturing and R&D
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology unveiled 'Semiconductor India 2.0', a revamped policy aimed at attracting further investments and fostering a robust domestic semiconductor ecosystem.
• The policy offers enhanced fiscal incentives, including capital expenditure subsidies, production-linked incentives (PLI), and tax breaks for companies setting up fabrication units and ATMP (Assembly, Test, Marking, and Packaging) facilities.
• A key focus is on developing indigenous R&D capabilities, promoting skill development in semiconductor design, and creating a talent pool through collaborations with academia.
• The government aims to position India as a global hub for semiconductor manufacturing and design, reducing reliance on imports and enhancing strategic autonomy.
• The policy also includes provisions for establishing 'Semiconductor Cities' with world-class infrastructure and a streamlined regulatory environment.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Semiconductor India 2.0 launched for domestic manufacturing.
Offers fiscal incentives, PLI, tax breaks.
Focus on R&D, skill development, 'Semiconductor Cities'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing a robust domestic semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem for India's economic and national security.
Analyze the key features of 'Semiconductor India 2.0' and evaluate its potential to attract global players and foster indigenous innovation.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in building a resilient semiconductor supply chain in India, considering global geopolitical dynamics.
articles.imageDescription: Microchip circuit board, symbolizing semiconductor technology.
articles.trivia: The global semiconductor industry is projected to exceed $1 trillion by 2030.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Make in India and PLI schemes

articles.headline: New Species of Orchid Discovered in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Hotspot's Richness
articles.explainer:
• Botanists from the Botanical Survey of India announced the discovery of a new species of orchid, *Dendrobium nilgirense*, in the biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The discovery underscores the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a global biodiversity hotspot and the need for its continued conservation.
• *Dendrobium nilgirense* is characterized by its unique floral morphology and specific habitat requirements, found only in a confined high-altitude evergreen forest patch.
• This finding emphasizes the ongoing threats to unexplored biodiversity due to habitat loss, climate change, and human encroachment.
• Researchers advocate for enhanced protection measures for critical habitats within the Western Ghats and further botanical surveys to document its rich flora.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: New orchid species, *Dendrobium nilgirense*, found in Western Ghats.
Discovery highlights Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot.
Emphasizes threats from habitat loss and climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the factors contributing to its unique flora and fauna.
Analyze the major threats to biodiversity in India, using the Western Ghats as a case study, and suggest comprehensive conservation strategies.
Evaluate the role of botanical surveys and scientific research in documenting and protecting India's natural heritage.
articles.imageDescription: Close-up image of the newly discovered *Dendrobium nilgirense* orchid.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

articles.headline: India-Bangladesh Joint River Management Commission Meets to Discuss Teesta and Feni River Water Sharing
articles.explainer:
• The India-Bangladesh Joint River Management Commission (JRMC) convened its annual meeting to discuss various transboundary river issues, including the long-pending Teesta water-sharing agreement.
• Both sides expressed commitment to finding an amicable solution for the equitable sharing of waters of the Teesta and Feni rivers, crucial for livelihoods in both nations.
• Discussions also covered flood forecasting, river pollution control, and joint projects for riverbank protection and navigation.
• The meeting highlighted the importance of water diplomacy in strengthening bilateral relations and addressing shared environmental challenges.
• A technical committee was formed to conduct further studies on water availability and usage patterns of the shared rivers.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability
prelimsPoints: India-Bangladesh JRMC met for Teesta and Feni river water sharing.
Focus on equitable sharing, flood forecasting, pollution control.
Technical committee formed for further studies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of transboundary river management for India's relations with its neighbours, particularly Bangladesh.
Analyze the complexities involved in the Teesta water-sharing dispute between India and Bangladesh and its implications for regional cooperation.
Evaluate the role of water diplomacy and joint commissions in resolving water-related disputes and promoting sustainable resource management.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the course of the Teesta River flowing through India and Bangladesh.
articles.trivia: India shares 54 transboundary rivers with Bangladesh.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Pashu Swasthya Card' - Digital Health ID for Livestock to Boost Animal Husbandry
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying launched the 'Pashu Swasthya Card' (Livestock Health Card) scheme, providing a digital health identity for cattle and other livestock.
• This initiative aims to improve animal health management, disease surveillance, and productivity in the animal husbandry sector.
• Each card will contain unique identification details, vaccination records, health history, and breeding information, accessible via a centralized digital platform.
• The scheme facilitates prompt veterinary services, reduces disease outbreaks, and helps farmers track their livestock's health and economic value.
• It is part of the broader 'National Digital Livestock Mission' to modernize the sector, enhance farmer income, and ensure food security.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Animal Rearing
Technology missions
prelimsPoints: Pashu Swasthya Card launched for digital health ID of livestock.
Aims to improve animal health management and productivity.
Part of 'National Digital Livestock Mission'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of digital technologies like the 'Pashu Swasthya Card' in transforming India's animal husbandry and dairy sectors.
Analyze how improved animal health management can contribute to increasing farmers' income and ensuring food security in India.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a nationwide digital livestock health system and suggest measures to overcome them.
articles.imageDescription: Farmer scanning a digital tag on a cow's ear.
articles.trivia: India has the world's largest livestock population, accounting for about 15% of the global livestock.

articles.headline: India's Highest Altitude Solar Power Plant Commissioned in Ladakh, Boosting Renewable Energy Capacity
articles.explainer:
• A new grid-connected solar power plant, India's highest-altitude installation, has been commissioned in Ladakh, significantly contributing to the region's renewable energy mix.
• Located at over 11,000 feet, the plant leverages Ladakh's abundant solar insolation to provide clean energy to remote areas and reduce reliance on diesel generators.
• The project involved overcoming extreme climatic conditions and logistical challenges, showcasing India's engineering capabilities in renewable energy infrastructure.
• This initiative is part of India's commitment to achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement and promoting sustainable development in ecologically sensitive regions.
• The plant also aims to provide energy security for military installations in the region and boost local economic development through green jobs.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Energy
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's highest altitude solar power plant commissioned in Ladakh.
Located at over 11,000 feet, reduces reliance on diesel.
Part of India's NDC commitment and sustainable development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and opportunities in developing renewable energy infrastructure in high-altitude and ecologically sensitive regions like Ladakh.
Analyze how such projects contribute to India's climate change commitments and energy security goals.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of localized renewable energy solutions on remote communities and military installations in border areas.
articles.imageDescription: Solar panels installed in a mountainous, snowy landscape in Ladakh.
articles.trivia: Ladakh is often called the 'cold desert' and receives intense solar radiation for over 300 days a year.

articles.headline: Ministry of I&B Releases New Guidelines for Online Content Platforms to Enhance Accountability
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Information and Broadcasting (I&B) released a new set of guidelines for online content platforms, including OTT services and digital news publishers.
• The guidelines aim to enhance accountability, transparency, and consumer protection in the digital media landscape.
• Key provisions include mandating a self-regulatory mechanism with an oversight body, establishing a grievance redressal system, and adhering to content classification norms.
• Platforms are required to provide clear age ratings and content advisories to users, empowering them to make informed choices.
• The move is intended to strike a balance between freedom of expression and the need for responsible content dissemination, addressing concerns about misinformation and harmful content.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability
prelimsPoints: New I&B Ministry guidelines for online content platforms.
Mandates self-regulatory mechanism, grievance redressal, content classification.
Aims for accountability, transparency, consumer protection.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind regulating online content platforms in India and the challenges in balancing freedom of expression with content moderation.
Analyze the key features of the new guidelines and evaluate their potential impact on OTT services, digital news, and user experience.
Critically examine the concept of self-regulation versus government oversight in the context of digital media and its implications for democracy.
articles.imageDescription: Smartphone screen displaying various OTT app logos.
articles.trivia: India is one of the fastest-growing OTT markets globally, with millions of new subscribers annually.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers Evidence of Earlier Settlement Layer at Harappan Site in Gujarat
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of an earlier, previously unknown settlement layer beneath a major Harappan site in Gujarat.
• This finding suggests a longer period of human occupation and potentially different cultural phases than previously understood for the region.
• Excavations revealed distinct pottery styles, architectural patterns, and artifacts indicating a pre-mature Harappan or early Harappan phase.
• The discovery could provide crucial insights into the evolution of the Indus Valley Civilization and its regional variations.
• Further research using advanced dating techniques and comparative studies is underway to establish the exact chronology and cultural affiliations of this new layer.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
History of India
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered earlier settlement layer at Harappan site in Gujarat.
Suggests longer occupation and distinct cultural phases.
Could provide insights into Indus Valley Civilization evolution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of new archaeological discoveries in reshaping our understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization.
Analyze the key characteristics of the Harappan civilization and how regional variations contribute to its complexity.
Evaluate the role of the Archaeological Survey of India in preserving and interpreting India's ancient cultural heritage.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists excavating a trench at a historical site.
articles.trivia: Dholavira, a prominent Harappan city in Gujarat, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

articles.headline: National Mental Health Survey Report Highlights Rising Mental Health Challenges Among Indian Youth
articles.explainer:
• The latest National Mental Health Survey report revealed a concerning rise in mental health issues, particularly anxiety and depression, among the youth in India.
• The survey attributed these trends to factors like academic pressure, social media influence, unemployment fears, and changing family structures.
• It highlighted significant gaps in mental health infrastructure, awareness, and accessibility of services, especially in rural areas.
• Recommendations include integrating mental health education into school curricula, strengthening counseling services, and leveraging digital platforms for outreach.
• The report calls for a multi-sectoral approach involving government, NGOs, educational institutions, and families to create a supportive environment for youth mental well-being.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India
Social empowerment
Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: National Mental Health Survey highlights rising issues among youth.
Factors include academic pressure, social media, unemployment fears.
Recommends integrating mental health education in schools.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major factors contributing to the rising mental health challenges among Indian youth and its implications for national development.
Analyze the existing gaps in mental health infrastructure and services in India, particularly for young people, and suggest policy interventions.
Evaluate the role of education, family, and digital platforms in fostering mental well-being and reducing stigma associated with mental health issues.
articles.imageDescription: Silhouette of a pensive young person against a cityscape.
articles.trivia: Globally, one in seven 10-19 year-olds experiences a mental disorder, accounting for 13% of the global burden of disease in this age group.

articles.headline: BRICS Finance Ministers Meet to Discuss Economic Cooperation, Digital Currencies, and Trade Expansion
articles.explainer:
• Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors from BRICS nations (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa, and new members) convened to discuss strategies for enhancing economic cooperation.
• Key agenda items included promoting trade in local currencies, exploring the feasibility of a common digital currency, and strengthening financial stability mechanisms.
• The discussions also focused on improving infrastructure financing through the New Development Bank (NDB) and addressing global economic challenges like inflation and supply chain disruptions.
• India emphasized the need for greater collaboration on digital public infrastructure and green finance initiatives within the BRICS framework.
• The meeting concluded with a joint statement reiterating commitment to multilateralism and a more inclusive global financial architecture.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: BRICS Finance Ministers met for economic cooperation.
Discussed local currencies, digital currency, NDB financing.
India pushed for digital public infrastructure and green finance.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving role of BRICS in the global economic landscape, particularly with the inclusion of new members.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of promoting trade in local currencies and exploring a common digital currency among BRICS nations.
Evaluate India's strategic interests and contributions within the BRICS framework, especially concerning digital public infrastructure and green finance.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of BRICS nations arranged around a conference table.
articles.trivia: BRICS was founded in 2009 as BRIC, with South Africa joining in 2010 to form BRICS.

articles.headline: Ichthyologists Discover New Species of Freshwater Fish in the Eastern Himalayas
articles.explainer:
• A team of ichthyologists announced the discovery of a new species of freshwater fish, *Garra ignesti*, in the remote streams of the Eastern Himalayas.
• The discovery highlights the rich and often unexplored aquatic biodiversity of the Himalayan region, which faces significant threats from climate change and infrastructure development.
• *Garra ignesti* is characterized by its unique morphological features, adapted to fast-flowing cold-water environments.
• Researchers emphasized the need for urgent conservation efforts to protect these pristine freshwater ecosystems and their endemic species.
• This finding contributes to the growing list of newly identified species from India, reinforcing its status as a mega-biodiverse country.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: New freshwater fish species, *Garra ignesti*, found in Eastern Himalayas.
Highlights rich aquatic biodiversity of the region.
Emphasizes need for conservation of freshwater ecosystems.
articles.imageDescription: Close-up image of a small freshwater fish in clear water.
articles.trivia: The Eastern Himalayas are part of one of the world's 36 biodiversity hotspots.

articles.headline: India's Position on Reforming UNSC Gains Traction Amidst Growing Calls for Global Governance Reforms
articles.explainer:
• India's long-standing demand for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) is gaining renewed traction among a growing number of member states.
• Several nations, including members of the G4 (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan), reiterated support for expanding both permanent and non-permanent categories of UNSC membership.
• India highlighted that the current structure of the UNSC, established in 1945, no longer reflects contemporary geopolitical realities, undermining its legitimacy and effectiveness.
• The discussions at the UN General Assembly focused on the need for a more representative, transparent, and accountable Security Council to address 21st-century global challenges.
• India emphasized its credentials as a responsible global power, a leading contributor to UN peacekeeping missions, and the world's largest democracy, deserving a permanent seat.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India's UNSC reform demand gaining traction.
G4 nations support expanding permanent and non-permanent seats.
India highlights current UNSC structure is outdated.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the reform of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) and India's role in this debate.
Analyze the implications of the current UNSC structure for global peace and security, and how a reformed council could address contemporary challenges more effectively.
Evaluate India's growing influence and its claim for a permanent seat in the UNSC, considering its contributions to international peace and development.
articles.imageDescription: UN Security Council chamber with flags of member states.
articles.trivia: The UN Security Council has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Records Significant Growth in Foreign Tourist Arrivals, Boosts Local Economies
articles.explainer:
• India's tourism sector reported a record increase in foreign tourist arrivals (FTAs) over the past year, significantly contributing to economic growth and job creation.
• The Ministry of Tourism attributed the surge to targeted promotional campaigns, improved infrastructure, and streamlined visa processes.
• States like Rajasthan, Kerala, and Goa witnessed particularly high growth, benefiting local economies through increased demand for hospitality, transport, and handicraft sectors.
• Medical tourism and eco-tourism segments also showed robust growth, attracting specialized visitors and promoting sustainable practices.
• The government announced further plans to enhance connectivity, develop new tourist circuits, and invest in skill development for the hospitality industry to sustain this momentum.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted impact of a flourishing tourism sector on India's economy, including job creation, foreign exchange earnings, and local development.
Analyze the strategies adopted by the Indian government to promote tourism, highlighting the success of targeted campaigns and infrastructure development.
Evaluate the potential of niche tourism segments like medical tourism and eco-tourism in driving sustainable and inclusive growth in India.
articles.imageDescription: Tourists visiting a historical monument in India.
articles.trivia: The World Travel & Tourism Council (WTTC) estimates that the travel and tourism sector contributes significantly to India's GDP and employment.

highlights: India Unveils National AI in Governance Strategy 2025, focusing on ethical deployment and public service delivery.
Supreme Court upholds 28% GST on online gaming, betting, and casinos, clarifying valuation on full face value of bets.
ISRO successfully hot tests a new high-thrust cryogenic engine (CE-30) for future heavy-lift launch missions.
India advocates for an equitable global framework on climate finance at the UN Climate Forum, emphasizing developed nations' responsibility.
Government launches 'Semiconductor India 2.0' policy with enhanced incentives to boost domestic manufacturing and R&D.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence in Governance (NSAIG) 2025', consider the following statements:
1. It aims to integrate AI across various government services to enhance efficiency and transparency.
2. It mandates the use of AI only in non-sensitive sectors to avoid data privacy concerns.
3. A dedicated National AI Council has been proposed to oversee its implementation.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as NSAIG 2025 aims to integrate AI for efficiency and transparency. Statement 2 is incorrect; the strategy focuses on ethical AI principles and data privacy *across* sectors, not restricting it to non-sensitive ones. Statement 3 is correct, as a National AI Council will oversee implementation.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the recent Supreme Court ruling on GST for online gaming, betting, and casinos?
options: (a) It has exempted skill-based online games from GST while taxing games of chance.
(b) It has upheld the imposition of 28% GST on the full face value of bets placed.
(c) It has reduced the GST rate for online gaming platforms to encourage the digital economy.
(d) It has introduced a new tax slab for online gaming based on the platform's net profit.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) It has upheld the imposition of 28% GST on the full face value of bets placed.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court upheld the 28% GST on online gaming, betting, and casinos, clarifying that the valuation would be on the full face value of the bets placed, not on net proceeds or platform fees. It did not exempt skill-based games from GST but rather clarified the taxation of 'actionable claims'.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about 'SLINEX 2025':
1. It is a bilateral maritime exercise between India and Sri Lanka.
2. The exercise focuses on enhancing cooperation in maritime security in the Bay of Bengal.
3. It included participation from maritime patrol aircraft and special forces.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; SLINEX is indeed a bilateral exercise between India and Sri Lanka. Statement 2 is partially correct but less precise; it focuses on maritime security in the broader Indian Ocean Region (IOR), not just the Bay of Bengal. Statement 3 is correct, as the exercise included naval ships, maritime patrol aircraft, and special forces.

prelimsMocks.question: With regard to India's climate finance stance at the UN Climate Forum, which of the following statements is INCORRECT?
options: (a) India emphasized the historical responsibility of developed nations.
(b) India advocated for a new, equitable global framework for climate finance.
(c) India stated that the $100 billion per year climate finance pledge has been adequately met.
(d) India pushed for transparent tracking of climate finance flows.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) India stated that the $100 billion per year climate finance pledge has been adequately met.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement (c) is incorrect. India stressed that existing climate finance commitments, particularly the $100 billion per year pledge, have *not* been adequately met, hindering global climate action. The other statements correctly reflect India's position.

prelimsMocks.question: The newly discovered orchid species *Dendrobium nilgirense* was found in which of the following regions of India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article states that botanists from the Botanical Survey of India announced the discovery of a new species of orchid, *Dendrobium nilgirense*, in the biodiverse Western Ghats region.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the potential of Artificial Intelligence in transforming public service delivery in India, while also discussing the ethical challenges and data privacy concerns associated with its widespread implementation, as addressed by the National AI in Governance Strategy 2025.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; Role of technology in governance.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the NSAIG 2025 and its objectives. Discuss the positive potential of AI in public service delivery (efficiency, transparency, accessibility, examples like healthcare, agriculture). Then, delve into the ethical challenges (algorithmic bias, discrimination, accountability) and data privacy concerns (data collection, storage, security, misuse). Conclude by suggesting measures for responsible AI deployment, linking back to the strategy's emphasis on human oversight and a dedicated AI council.

mainsMocks.question: Examine the strategic importance of developing a robust domestic semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem for India's economic and national security. Discuss the key features of 'Semiconductor India 2.0' policy and evaluate its potential to achieve the stated objectives.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by highlighting the criticality of semiconductors in modern economy and strategic sectors. Explain why domestic manufacturing is vital for economic resilience, strategic autonomy, and reducing import dependence. Detail the key features of 'Semiconductor India 2.0' (fiscal incentives, PLI, R&D, skill development, 'Semiconductor Cities'). Evaluate its potential by discussing opportunities (attracting investment, job creation, innovation) and challenges (high capital costs, technology gaps, global competition, geopolitical risks). Conclude with a forward-looking perspective on India's prospects.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss India's long-standing demand for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC). Analyze the arguments for and against expanding its permanent and non-permanent categories, and evaluate India's credentials for a permanent seat.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the UNSC and India's consistent call for reforms, highlighting its outdated structure. Present arguments for reform (lack of representativeness, changed geopolitical landscape, effectiveness issues) and arguments against (veto power dilution, regional rivalries, difficulty in consensus). Detail India's credentials for a permanent seat (economic power, democracy, population, UN peacekeeping contributions, responsible nuclear power). Conclude with the broader implications of UNSC reform for global governance and India's rising stature.

quickBytes.text: The 'Pashu Swasthya Card' scheme provides a digital health ID for livestock, aimed at improving animal health management.
SLINEX 2025 is the 13th edition of the bilateral maritime exercise between India and Sri Lanka.
The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are a recognized global biodiversity hotspot.
Cryogenic engines use liquid oxygen and liquid hydrogen as propellants, stored at extremely low temperatures.
India shares 54 transboundary rivers with Bangladesh, making water diplomacy crucial.
The $100 billion climate finance goal was first pledged by developed countries at COP15 in Copenhagen (2009).
The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks in case of fund shortfalls.
The Disaster Management Act was enacted in India in 2005.
Gyalo Thondup authored the memoir 'The Noodle Maker of Kalimpong', detailing his life in exile.
India has the world's largest livestock population, accounting for about 15% of the global total.

GS1.headline: Gyalo Thondup, Elder Brother of 14th Dalai Lama, Passes Away at 97
GS1.explainer:
• Gyalo Thondup, the elder brother of the 14th Dalai Lama, Tenzin Gyatso, passed away at the age of 97.
• He was a prominent figure in the Tibetan exile community and played a crucial role in early diplomatic efforts for Tibet after the Chinese invasion in 1950.
• Thondup served as the head of the Tibetan government-in-exile's foreign department for many years, engaging with international leaders and organizations.
• He was known for his pragmatic approach and his efforts to maintain communication channels with Beijing, advocating for a peaceful resolution to the Tibet issue.
• His extensive memoirs, 'The Noodle Maker of Kalimpong', offer unique insights into Tibetan history and the challenges faced by the exiled community.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues
India and its neighbourhood- relations
prelimsPoints: Gyalo Thondup was the elder brother of the 14th Dalai Lama.
Key figure in the Tibetan government-in-exile's foreign department.
Authored 'The Noodle Maker of Kalimpong'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical role of the Tibetan government-in-exile in preserving Tibetan culture and identity.
Analyze the complexities of India's policy towards Tibet and its implications for India-China relations.
Examine the 'Middle Way Approach' advocated by the Dalai Lama and its relevance in resolving the Tibet issue.
GS1.imageDescription: Portrait of Gyalo Thondup in his later years.
GS1.trivia: Kalimpong, mentioned in his memoir title, is a town in West Bengal, India, which became a significant hub for Tibetan refugees.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers Evidence of Earlier Settlement Layer at Harappan Site in Gujarat
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of an earlier, previously unknown settlement layer beneath a major Harappan site in Gujarat.
• This finding suggests a longer period of human occupation and potentially different cultural phases than previously understood for the region.
• Excavations revealed distinct pottery styles, architectural patterns, and artifacts indicating a pre-mature Harappan or early Harappan phase.
• The discovery could provide crucial insights into the evolution of the Indus Valley Civilization and its regional variations.
• Further research using advanced dating techniques and comparative studies is underway to establish the exact chronology and cultural affiliations of this new layer.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
History of India
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered earlier settlement layer at Harappan site in Gujarat.
Suggests longer occupation and distinct cultural phases.
Could provide insights into Indus Valley Civilization evolution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of new archaeological discoveries in reshaping our understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization.
Analyze the key characteristics of the Harappan civilization and how regional variations contribute to its complexity.
Evaluate the role of the Archaeological Survey of India in preserving and interpreting India's ancient cultural heritage.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists excavating a trench at a historical site.
GS1.trivia: Dholavira, a prominent Harappan city in Gujarat, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

GS1.headline: National Mental Health Survey Report Highlights Rising Mental Health Challenges Among Indian Youth
GS1.explainer:
• The latest National Mental Health Survey report revealed a concerning rise in mental health issues, particularly anxiety and depression, among the youth in India.
• The survey attributed these trends to factors like academic pressure, social media influence, unemployment fears, and changing family structures.
• It highlighted significant gaps in mental health infrastructure, awareness, and accessibility of services, especially in rural areas.
• Recommendations include integrating mental health education into school curricula, strengthening counseling services, and leveraging digital platforms for outreach.
• The report calls for a multi-sectoral approach involving government, NGOs, educational institutions, and families to create a supportive environment for youth mental well-being.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India
Social empowerment
Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
GS1.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: National Mental Health Survey highlights rising issues among youth.
Factors include academic pressure, social media, unemployment fears.
Recommends integrating mental health education in schools.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major factors contributing to the rising mental health challenges among Indian youth and its implications for national development.
Analyze the existing gaps in mental health infrastructure and services in India, particularly for young people, and suggest policy interventions.
Evaluate the role of education, family, and digital platforms in fostering mental well-being and reducing stigma associated with mental health issues.
GS1.imageDescription: Silhouette of a pensive young person against a cityscape.
GS1.trivia: Globally, one in seven 10-19 year-olds experiences a mental disorder, accounting for 13% of the global burden of disease in this age group.

GS2.headline: India Unveils National AI in Governance Strategy 2025: Focus on Ethical Deployment and Public Service Delivery
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the 'National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence in Governance (NSAIG) 2025', aiming to integrate AI across various government services.
• Focus areas include enhancing efficiency, transparency, and accessibility of public services, with a strong emphasis on ethical AI principles and data privacy.
• The strategy outlines a framework for responsible AI development, including guidelines for data acquisition, algorithm bias detection, and human oversight.
• Key sectors identified for initial implementation include healthcare, education, agriculture, and urban planning.
• A dedicated National AI Council, comprising experts from government, industry, and academia, will oversee the strategy's implementation and evolution.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Role of technology in governance
prelimsPoints: NSAIG 2025 launched for AI integration in government.
National AI Council established for oversight.
Initial focus sectors: healthcare, education, agriculture, urban planning.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of AI in transforming public service delivery in India, citing examples from NSAIG 2025.
Analyze the challenges of implementing AI in governance, particularly concerning ethical considerations, data privacy, and algorithmic bias.
Evaluate the role of a dedicated National AI Council in ensuring responsible AI development and deployment.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of AI algorithms connecting various government services.
GS2.trivia: India is among the top 10 countries globally in terms of AI skill penetration.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Applications of AI in public administration

GS2.headline: India-Sri Lanka Joint Naval Exercise 'SLINEX 2025' Concludes, Enhancing Maritime Security Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The 13th edition of the bilateral maritime exercise 'SLINEX 2025' between India and Sri Lanka concluded in Visakhapatnam, focusing on interoperability and mutual understanding.
• The exercise included naval ships, maritime patrol aircraft, and special forces from both nations, conducting a range of drills from anti-piracy operations to HADR (Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief).
• SLINEX aims to strengthen the enduring friendship and enhance cooperation in maritime security in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• Discussions also focused on sharing best practices in maritime domain awareness (MDA) and addressing common challenges like illegal fishing and maritime terrorism.
• The exercise underscores India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and its commitment to Sagar (Security and Growth for All in the Region) doctrine.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: SLINEX is a bilateral maritime exercise between India and Sri Lanka.
13th edition concluded in Visakhapatnam.
Focus on HADR, anti-piracy, and MDA.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India-Sri Lanka defence cooperation for maritime security in the Indian Ocean Region.
Discuss how India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy is reflected in its defence engagements with littoral states.
Examine the common maritime security challenges faced by India and Sri Lanka and how joint exercises like SLINEX help address them.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and Sri Lankan naval ships sailing in formation during SLINEX.
GS2.trivia: Sri Lanka is strategically located along major international shipping lanes in the Indian Ocean.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Reforms in Disaster Management Act, 2005
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report recommending significant amendments to the Disaster Management Act, 2005, to address emerging challenges.
• Key recommendations include integrating climate change adaptation and mitigation into disaster management planning and strengthening early warning systems.
• The panel called for greater emphasis on pre-disaster preparedness, risk reduction, and community-led initiatives, moving beyond a reactive relief-centric approach.
• It also suggested streamlining the National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) and State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) operations and improving inter-agency coordination.
• The report highlighted the need for a dedicated fund for disaster mitigation and capacity building at the local level, along with robust post-disaster recovery frameworks.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary panel recommended reforms for DMA 2005.
Focus on climate change, pre-disaster preparedness, community-led initiatives.
Suggested dedicated fund for mitigation and capacity building.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the effectiveness of the Disaster Management Act, 2005, in addressing contemporary disaster challenges in India.
Discuss the importance of shifting from a reactive to a proactive and holistic approach in disaster management, as recommended by the Parliamentary Panel.
Examine the role of community participation and local capacity building in enhancing disaster resilience in India.
GS2.imageDescription: People evacuating during a flood, depicting disaster management scenario.
GS2.trivia: India is highly vulnerable to various natural disasters, with over 58% of its landmass prone to earthquakes.

GS2.headline: India Pushes for Equitable Global Framework on Climate Finance at UN Climate Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India advocated for a new, equitable global framework for climate finance at the ongoing UN Climate Forum, emphasizing the historical responsibility of developed nations.
• The Indian delegation highlighted the need for increased and predictable financial support for developing countries to achieve their climate mitigation and adaptation goals.
• India stressed that existing climate finance commitments, particularly the $100 billion per year pledge, have not been adequately met, hindering global climate action.
• The proposal included mechanisms for transparent tracking of climate finance flows and ensuring that funds are easily accessible and do not add to the debt burden of developing nations.
• This push aligns with India's long-standing position on 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in climate negotiations.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Environmental pollution and degradation
keyTerms: Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: India advocated for equitable global climate finance framework.
Emphasized historical responsibility of developed nations.
Highlighted failure to meet $100 billion climate finance pledge.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's stance on climate finance at international forums and the principles underlying its demands.
Analyze the challenges in mobilizing and disbursing adequate climate finance to developing countries and the implications for global climate action.
Evaluate the concept of 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in the context of global climate negotiations and its relevance today.
GS2.imageDescription: Delegates discussing at a UN climate conference table.
GS2.trivia: The $100 billion climate finance goal was first pledged by developed countries at COP15 in Copenhagen in 2009.

GS2.headline: India-Bangladesh Joint River Management Commission Meets to Discuss Teesta and Feni River Water Sharing
GS2.explainer:
• The India-Bangladesh Joint River Management Commission (JRMC) convened its annual meeting to discuss various transboundary river issues, including the long-pending Teesta water-sharing agreement.
• Both sides expressed commitment to finding an amicable solution for the equitable sharing of waters of the Teesta and Feni rivers, crucial for livelihoods in both nations.
• Discussions also covered flood forecasting, river pollution control, and joint projects for riverbank protection and navigation.
• The meeting highlighted the importance of water diplomacy in strengthening bilateral relations and addressing shared environmental challenges.
• A technical committee was formed to conduct further studies on water availability and usage patterns of the shared rivers.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability
prelimsPoints: India-Bangladesh JRMC met for Teesta and Feni river water sharing.
Focus on equitable sharing, flood forecasting, pollution control.
Technical committee formed for further studies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of transboundary river management for India's relations with its neighbours, particularly Bangladesh.
Analyze the complexities involved in the Teesta water-sharing dispute between India and Bangladesh and its implications for regional cooperation.
Evaluate the role of water diplomacy and joint commissions in resolving water-related disputes and promoting sustainable resource management.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing the course of the Teesta River flowing through India and Bangladesh.
GS2.trivia: India shares 54 transboundary rivers with Bangladesh.

GS2.headline: Ministry of I&B Releases New Guidelines for Online Content Platforms to Enhance Accountability
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Information and Broadcasting (I&B) released a new set of guidelines for online content platforms, including OTT services and digital news publishers.
• The guidelines aim to enhance accountability, transparency, and consumer protection in the digital media landscape.
• Key provisions include mandating a self-regulatory mechanism with an oversight body, establishing a grievance redressal system, and adhering to content classification norms.
• Platforms are required to provide clear age ratings and content advisories to users, empowering them to make informed choices.
• The move is intended to strike a balance between freedom of expression and the need for responsible content dissemination, addressing concerns about misinformation and harmful content.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability
prelimsPoints: New I&B Ministry guidelines for online content platforms.
Mandates self-regulatory mechanism, grievance redressal, content classification.
Aims for accountability, transparency, consumer protection.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind regulating online content platforms in India and the challenges in balancing freedom of expression with content moderation.
Analyze the key features of the new guidelines and evaluate their potential impact on OTT services, digital news, and user experience.
Critically examine the concept of self-regulation versus government oversight in the context of digital media and its implications for democracy.
GS2.imageDescription: Smartphone screen displaying various OTT app logos.
GS2.trivia: India is one of the fastest-growing OTT markets globally, with millions of new subscribers annually.

GS2.headline: BRICS Finance Ministers Meet to Discuss Economic Cooperation, Digital Currencies, and Trade Expansion
GS2.explainer:
• Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors from BRICS nations (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa, and new members) convened to discuss strategies for enhancing economic cooperation.
• Key agenda items included promoting trade in local currencies, exploring the feasibility of a common digital currency, and strengthening financial stability mechanisms.
• The discussions also focused on improving infrastructure financing through the New Development Bank (NDB) and addressing global economic challenges like inflation and supply chain disruptions.
• India emphasized the need for greater collaboration on digital public infrastructure and green finance initiatives within the BRICS framework.
• The meeting concluded with a joint statement reiterating commitment to multilateralism and a more inclusive global financial architecture.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: BRICS Finance Ministers met for economic cooperation.
Discussed local currencies, digital currency, NDB financing.
India pushed for digital public infrastructure and green finance.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving role of BRICS in the global economic landscape, particularly with the inclusion of new members.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of promoting trade in local currencies and exploring a common digital currency among BRICS nations.
Evaluate India's strategic interests and contributions within the BRICS framework, especially concerning digital public infrastructure and green finance.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of BRICS nations arranged around a conference table.
GS2.trivia: BRICS was founded in 2009 as BRIC, with South Africa joining in 2010 to form BRICS.

GS2.headline: India's Position on Reforming UNSC Gains Traction Amidst Growing Calls for Global Governance Reforms
GS2.explainer:
• India's long-standing demand for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) is gaining renewed traction among a growing number of member states.
• Several nations, including members of the G4 (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan), reiterated support for expanding both permanent and non-permanent categories of UNSC membership.
• India highlighted that the current structure of the UNSC, established in 1945, no longer reflects contemporary geopolitical realities, undermining its legitimacy and effectiveness.
• The discussions at the UN General Assembly focused on the need for a more representative, transparent, and accountable Security Council to address 21st-century global challenges.
• India emphasized its credentials as a responsible global power, a leading contributor to UN peacekeeping missions, and the world's largest democracy, deserving a permanent seat.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India's UNSC reform demand gaining traction.
G4 nations support expanding permanent and non-permanent seats.
India highlights current UNSC structure is outdated.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the reform of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) and India's role in this debate.
Analyze the implications of the current UNSC structure for global peace and security, and how a reformed council could address contemporary challenges more effectively.
Evaluate India's growing influence and its claim for a permanent seat in the UNSC, considering its contributions to international peace and development.
GS2.imageDescription: UN Security Council chamber with flags of member states.
GS2.trivia: The UN Security Council has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.

GS3.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of GST on Online Gaming, Betting and Casinos
GS3.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India upheld the validity of the amendments to the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Act, 2017, imposing 28% GST on online gaming, betting, and casinos.
• The ruling clarified that the valuation for GST would be on the full face value of the bets placed, not on the net proceeds or platform fees.
• This decision brings clarity to the taxation regime for the burgeoning online gaming industry, which had challenged the retrospective application and valuation method.
• The court emphasized the legislative intent to treat these activities as 'actionable claims' for taxation purposes, distinct from skill-based games for revenue generation.
• The judgment is expected to significantly impact the revenue collection for the government and may lead to consolidation in the online gaming sector.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: SC upheld 28% GST on online gaming, betting, casinos.
GST valuation on full face value of bets.
Activities treated as 'actionable claims' for taxation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's decision on the Indian online gaming industry and its impact on revenue generation for the government.
Discuss the debate surrounding the distinction between 'games of skill' and 'games of chance' in the context of taxation and regulation in India.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities presented by the taxation of the digital economy, using the online gaming sector as an example.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating GST percentage on online gaming with court gavel.
GS3.trivia: The global online gaming market is projected to reach over $300 billion by 2027.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Cryogenic Engine for Future Heavy-Lift Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful hot test of its new high-thrust cryogenic engine, designed for future heavy-lift launch vehicles.
• This engine, designated 'CE-30', is a significant upgrade from the existing CE-20 used in GSLV Mk-III (LVM3), offering increased thrust and efficiency.
• The successful test marks a crucial milestone in India's ambition to enhance its capabilities for launching heavier communication satellites and deep-space missions.
• The CE-30 engine incorporates advanced materials and design methodologies, improving propellant efficiency and reducing overall launch costs.
• This development is vital for India's manned space mission, Gaganyaan, and for establishing a more robust presence in the commercial space launch market.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully hot-tested 'CE-30' cryogenic engine.
CE-30 is a next-gen engine for heavy-lift missions.
Significant for Gaganyaan and commercial space launches.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous cryogenic engine technology for India's space program and its strategic implications.
Analyze how advancements in launch vehicle technology, like the CE-30 engine, contribute to India's aspirations in deep-space exploration and manned missions.
Evaluate the potential impact of enhanced heavy-lift capabilities on India's position in the global commercial space market.
GS3.imageDescription: Diagram of a cryogenic engine undergoing a hot test.
GS3.trivia: Cryogenic engines use propellants (liquid oxygen and liquid hydrogen) stored at extremely low temperatures.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns and Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) maintained the benchmark repo rate at 6.5% for the seventh consecutive time.
• The decision was driven by persistent concerns over elevated retail inflation, particularly in food prices, and global economic uncertainties.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• The MPC's outlook highlighted risks from geopolitical tensions, volatility in crude oil prices, and potential impact on domestic economic activity.
• Analysts anticipate that the RBI will continue to adopt a cautious stance, closely monitoring inflation trajectory and global developments before considering any rate adjustments.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Monetary Policy
Inflation
prelimsPoints: RBI maintained repo rate at 6.5%.
Seventh consecutive time rate held steady.
Decision driven by inflation concerns and global uncertainties.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst current economic conditions, including inflation and global factors.
Discuss the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance of the RBI and its implications for economic growth and inflation control.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation management with the need to support economic recovery.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI emblem over a graph showing stable repo rate.
GS3.trivia: The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks in the event of any shortfall of funds.

GS3.headline: Government Launches 'Semiconductor India 2.0' Policy to Boost Domestic Manufacturing and R&D
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology unveiled 'Semiconductor India 2.0', a revamped policy aimed at attracting further investments and fostering a robust domestic semiconductor ecosystem.
• The policy offers enhanced fiscal incentives, including capital expenditure subsidies, production-linked incentives (PLI), and tax breaks for companies setting up fabrication units and ATMP (Assembly, Test, Marking, and Packaging) facilities.
• A key focus is on developing indigenous R&D capabilities, promoting skill development in semiconductor design, and creating a talent pool through collaborations with academia.
• The government aims to position India as a global hub for semiconductor manufacturing and design, reducing reliance on imports and enhancing strategic autonomy.
• The policy also includes provisions for establishing 'Semiconductor Cities' with world-class infrastructure and a streamlined regulatory environment.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Semiconductor India 2.0 launched for domestic manufacturing.
Offers fiscal incentives, PLI, tax breaks.
Focus on R&D, skill development, 'Semiconductor Cities'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing a robust domestic semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem for India's economic and national security.
Analyze the key features of 'Semiconductor India 2.0' and evaluate its potential to attract global players and foster indigenous innovation.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in building a resilient semiconductor supply chain in India, considering global geopolitical dynamics.
GS3.imageDescription: Microchip circuit board, symbolizing semiconductor technology.
GS3.trivia: The global semiconductor industry is projected to exceed $1 trillion by 2030.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Make in India and PLI schemes

GS3.headline: New Species of Orchid Discovered in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Hotspot's Richness
GS3.explainer:
• Botanists from the Botanical Survey of India announced the discovery of a new species of orchid, *Dendrobium nilgirense*, in the biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The discovery underscores the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a global biodiversity hotspot and the need for its continued conservation.
• *Dendrobium nilgirense* is characterized by its unique floral morphology and specific habitat requirements, found only in a confined high-altitude evergreen forest patch.
• This finding emphasizes the ongoing threats to unexplored biodiversity due to habitat loss, climate change, and human encroachment.
• Researchers advocate for enhanced protection measures for critical habitats within the Western Ghats and further botanical surveys to document its rich flora.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: New orchid species, *Dendrobium nilgirense*, found in Western Ghats.
Discovery highlights Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot.
Emphasizes threats from habitat loss and climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the factors contributing to its unique flora and fauna.
Analyze the major threats to biodiversity in India, using the Western Ghats as a case study, and suggest comprehensive conservation strategies.
Evaluate the role of botanical surveys and scientific research in documenting and protecting India's natural heritage.
GS3.imageDescription: Close-up image of the newly discovered *Dendrobium nilgirense* orchid.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

GS3.headline: Government Launches 'Pashu Swasthya Card' - Digital Health ID for Livestock to Boost Animal Husbandry
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying launched the 'Pashu Swasthya Card' (Livestock Health Card) scheme, providing a digital health identity for cattle and other livestock.
• This initiative aims to improve animal health management, disease surveillance, and productivity in the animal husbandry sector.
• Each card will contain unique identification details, vaccination records, health history, and breeding information, accessible via a centralized digital platform.
• The scheme facilitates prompt veterinary services, reduces disease outbreaks, and helps farmers track their livestock's health and economic value.
