• It is part of the broader 'National Digital Livestock Mission' to modernize the sector, enhance farmer income, and ensure food security.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Animal Rearing
Technology missions
prelimsPoints: Pashu Swasthya Card launched for digital health ID of livestock.
Aims to improve animal health management and productivity.
Part of 'National Digital Livestock Mission'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of digital technologies like the 'Pashu Swasthya Card' in transforming India's animal husbandry and dairy sectors.
Analyze how improved animal health management can contribute to increasing farmers' income and ensuring food security in India.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a nationwide digital livestock health system and suggest measures to overcome them.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmer scanning a digital tag on a cow's ear.
GS3.trivia: India has the world's largest livestock population, accounting for about 15% of the global livestock.

GS3.headline: India's Highest Altitude Solar Power Plant Commissioned in Ladakh, Boosting Renewable Energy Capacity
GS3.explainer:
• A new grid-connected solar power plant, India's highest-altitude installation, has been commissioned in Ladakh, significantly contributing to the region's renewable energy mix.
• Located at over 11,000 feet, the plant leverages Ladakh's abundant solar insolation to provide clean energy to remote areas and reduce reliance on diesel generators.
• The project involved overcoming extreme climatic conditions and logistical challenges, showcasing India's engineering capabilities in renewable energy infrastructure.
• This initiative is part of India's commitment to achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement and promoting sustainable development in ecologically sensitive regions.
• The plant also aims to provide energy security for military installations in the region and boost local economic development through green jobs.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Energy
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's highest altitude solar power plant commissioned in Ladakh.
Located at over 11,000 feet, reduces reliance on diesel.
Part of India's NDC commitment and sustainable development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and opportunities in developing renewable energy infrastructure in high-altitude and ecologically sensitive regions like Ladakh.
Analyze how such projects contribute to India's climate change commitments and energy security goals.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of localized renewable energy solutions on remote communities and military installations in border areas.
GS3.imageDescription: Solar panels installed in a mountainous, snowy landscape in Ladakh.
GS3.trivia: Ladakh is often called the 'cold desert' and receives intense solar radiation for over 300 days a year.

GS3.headline: Ichthyologists Discover New Species of Freshwater Fish in the Eastern Himalayas
GS3.explainer:
• A team of ichthyologists announced the discovery of a new species of freshwater fish, *Garra ignesti*, in the remote streams of the Eastern Himalayas.
• The discovery highlights the rich and often unexplored aquatic biodiversity of the Himalayan region, which faces significant threats from climate change and infrastructure development.
• *Garra ignesti* is characterized by its unique morphological features, adapted to fast-flowing cold-water environments.
• Researchers emphasized the need for urgent conservation efforts to protect these pristine freshwater ecosystems and their endemic species.
• This finding contributes to the growing list of newly identified species from India, reinforcing its status as a mega-biodiverse country.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: New freshwater fish species, *Garra ignesti*, found in Eastern Himalayas.
Highlights rich aquatic biodiversity of the region.
Emphasizes need for conservation of freshwater ecosystems.
GS3.imageDescription: Close-up image of a small freshwater fish in clear water.
GS3.trivia: The Eastern Himalayas are part of one of the world's 36 biodiversity hotspots.

GS3.headline: India's Tourism Sector Records Significant Growth in Foreign Tourist Arrivals, Boosts Local Economies
GS3.explainer:
• India's tourism sector reported a record increase in foreign tourist arrivals (FTAs) over the past year, significantly contributing to economic growth and job creation.
• The Ministry of Tourism attributed the surge to targeted promotional campaigns, improved infrastructure, and streamlined visa processes.
• States like Rajasthan, Kerala, and Goa witnessed particularly high growth, benefiting local economies through increased demand for hospitality, transport, and handicraft sectors.
• Medical tourism and eco-tourism segments also showed robust growth, attracting specialized visitors and promoting sustainable practices.
• The government announced further plans to enhance connectivity, develop new tourist circuits, and invest in skill development for the hospitality industry to sustain this momentum.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted impact of a flourishing tourism sector on India's economy, including job creation, foreign exchange earnings, and local development.
Analyze the strategies adopted by the Indian government to promote tourism, highlighting the success of targeted campaigns and infrastructure development.
Evaluate the potential of niche tourism segments like medical tourism and eco-tourism in driving sustainable and inclusive growth in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Tourists visiting a historical monument in India.
GS3.trivia: The World Travel & Tourism Council (WTTC) estimates that the travel and tourism sector contributes significantly to India's GDP and employment.
